Results.Query: Write an apology letter to a friend whose birthday I missed
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The user is asking me to write an apology letter. This is a creative writing/conversational task — Domain A. No need to search Wikipedia.
</thinking>

Here's a heartfelt apology letter you can personalize and send to your friend:

---

**Subject: I'm So Sorry I Missed Your Birthday**

Dear [Friend's Name],

I hope you're doing well. I'm writing this with a heavy heart because I owe you a sincere and long-overdue apology — I missed your birthday, and that was completely unacceptable on my part.

There is no excuse good enough to justify it. You are someone who has always shown up for me, and the least I could have done was show up for you on your special day. I let you down, and I am truly, deeply sorry.

Your birthday is a day that deserves to be celebrated, and you deserve to feel valued and remembered by the people who care about you. I failed to do that, and I can only imagine how that must have felt. Please know that my missing it had nothing to do with how much you mean to me — you mean the world to me, and I am ashamed that my actions didn't reflect that.

I'd love the chance to make it up to you. Whether it's a belated celebration over dinner, coffee, or whatever you'd enjoy, I want to take the time to properly honor you and show you how much your friendship means to me. You deserve nothing less.

I understand if you're upset, and I respect whatever you're feeling right now. I just hope you'll give me the opportunity to do better, because you are one of the most important people in my life and I never want you to feel forgotten or unappreciated.

Once again, I am so sorry. Happy belated birthday, [Friend's Name]. I hope it was filled with joy, love, and everything you deserved — even without me there.

With love and sincere apologies,
[Your Name]

---

**Tips to personalize it:**
- 📝 **Add a specific memory** — mention a favorite moment you've shared to remind them of your bond.
- 🎁 **Include a plan** — if you already have a make-up celebration in mind, mention it specifically.
- 🗓️ **Acknowledge the gap** — if a lot of time has passed since their birthday, acknowledge it directly so it doesn't seem like an afterthought.
- ✍️ **Handwrite it** — a handwritten letter or card always feels more personal and sincere than a text or email.

Results.Query: Who was the first president of the United States?
Results.ActualAnswer: **George Washington** was the first president of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797. He was also a Founding Father and served as commander of the Continental Army. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington)

Results.Query: When was the Eiffel Tower built?
Results.ActualAnswer: The Eiffel Tower was constructed **from 1887 to 1889** by the company of engineer Gustave Eiffel, after whom it is named [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower). It was built for the 1889 World's Fair in Paris, France [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gustave_Eiffel).

Results.Query: What is the population of Tokyo?
Results.ActualAnswer: Tokyo, officially the **Tokyo Metropolis**, is the capital and most populous city of Japan. As of 2023, it has a population of over **14 million** in the city proper [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo), with a more precise figure of **14,195,730** recorded in its demographics [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Tokyo), representing about **11.5% of Japan's total population** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Tokyo).

Results.ActualAnswer: **Russia** is the largest country in the world by land area, covering over **17,125,191 square kilometers** (6,612,073 sq mi), encompassing more than one-eighth of Earth's inhabited land area. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geography_of_Russia)

Results.Query: What year did World War II end?
Results.ActualAnswer:
World War II ended in **1945**, though in two stages:

- **In Europe**: The war ended in **May 1945**, following the defeat of Nazi Germany [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Europe).
- **In Asia**: The war officially concluded on **September 2, 1945**, with Japan's formal surrender aboard the USS Missouri [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Asia).

September 2, 1945 is generally recognized as the official end date of the entire war.

Results.Query: Who invented the telephone?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**Alexander Graham Bell** is credited with patenting the first practical telephone, receiving the first U.S. patent for it on March 7, 1876 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell). He was a Scottish-born inventor, scientist, and engineer who also co-founded AT&T [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell).

However, the invention of the telephone is frequently disputed. Key figures involved in its development include **Antonio Meucci**, **Philipp Reis**, and **Elisha Gray**, in addition to Bell [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Invention_of_the_telephone). The U.S. House of Representatives passed a resolution in 2002 acknowledging **Antonio Meucci's** contributions to the invention of the telephone, though the U.S. Senate did not join the resolution [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci).

Results.Query: What is the speed of light?
Results.ActualAnswer: The speed of light in vacuum, commonly denoted **c**, is a universal physical constant exactly equal to **299,792,458 metres per second (m·s⁻¹)** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light). This value is exact because the metre is itself defined in terms of the speed of light.

Results.Query: Tell me about Python
Results.ActualAnswer:
"Python" refers to multiple distinct things. Could you clarify which one you mean? Here are the two most common:

---

### 🐍 Python (Snake)
Pythons (family **Pythonidae**) are nonvenomous snakes found in Africa, Asia, and Australia [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pythonidae). They are among the largest snakes in the world — for example, the **Reticulated python** (*Malayopython reticulatus*) is the world's longest snake [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reticulated_python), and the **Burmese python** (*Python bivittatus*) is one of the largest species, native to Southeast Asia and listed as Vulnerable [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Burmese_python).

---

### 💻 Python (Programming Language)
**Python** is a high-level, general-purpose programming language that emphasizes code readability, simplicity, and ease-of-writing [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language)). It was started by **Guido van Rossum** in **1989** and first released in **1991** [5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Outline_of_the_Python_programming_language). It supports multiple paradigms including functional programming and is widely used in data science, web development, automation, and more.

---

Please let me know which **Python** you'd like to learn more about!

Results.Query: Did Einstein win a Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**No.** Einstein did not win the Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity. He was awarded the **1921 Nobel Prize in Physics** "for his services to Theoretical Physics, and especially for his **discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect**" [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_awards_and_honors_received_by_Albert_Einstein) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photoelectric_effect).

While Einstein is most famous for developing the **theory of relativity** [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein), that work was notably *not* the basis for his Nobel Prize. The photoelectric effect — which described how light ejects electrons from a surface and was foundational to quantum theory — was the specific discovery the Nobel Committee recognized.

Results.Query: Did Napoleon win the Battle of Waterloo?
Results.ActualAnswer: No, Napoleon did **not** win the Battle of Waterloo. The Seventh Coalition — led by the Duke of Wellington and supported by a Prussian army — defeated Napoleon at the battle [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arthur_Wellesley,_1st_Duke_of_Wellington). Following the French defeat, Napoleon left for Paris [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waterloo_campaign), and the battle effectively ended both the Napoleonic Wars and Napoleon's rule, leading to his final abdication and exile to Saint Helena.

Results.Query: Did the Titanic sink on its first voyage?
Results.ActualAnswer: Yes. RMS Titanic sank during its **maiden (first) voyage**. It struck an iceberg and sank in the early hours of **15 April 1912**, while traveling from Southampton [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Titanic). An estimated 2,240 people were on board for that first and only voyage [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crew_of_the_Titanic).

Results.Query: Who won the 2025 FIFA World Cup?
Results.ActualAnswer:
There is no **2025 FIFA World Cup** (the men's national team tournament). The FIFA World Cup is held every four years; the next edition is the **2026 FIFA World Cup**, which has not yet taken place. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup)

You may be thinking of one of these related 2025 tournaments:
- **2025 FIFA Club World Cup** – a competition between international club teams. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_FIFA_Club_World_Cup)
- **2025 FIFA U-17 World Cup** – for under-17 national teams. [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_FIFA_U-17_World_Cup)

Results.Query: Who was the French president when the Eiffel Tower was built?
Results.ActualAnswer: The Eiffel Tower was built from **1887 to 1889** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower). During that entire period, **Sadi Carnot** (Marie-François Sadi Carnot) served as President of France, having taken office in 1887 and serving until his assassination in 1894 [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadi_Carnot_(statesman)).

Results.Query: What team did Michael Jordan play for when he won his first NBA championship?
Results.ActualAnswer: Michael Jordan won his first NBA championship with the **Chicago Bulls** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Jordan). He went on to win a total of six championships with the Bulls, securing his first title in 1991.
